paper_title: No tephra in Greenland from the historic eruption of Vesuvius in 79 CE

methods.method_name: Ice Core Sample Preparation and Tephra Analysis
methods.description: Process for preparing ice core samples for tephra analysis, including cutting, transferring, and analyzing samples to identify tephra content.

steps.description: Three annual ice samples bracketing a sulfate peak at 410.56 m in the NEEM-2011-S1 core were cut for tephra analysis between 410.20 and 410.85 m using a bandsaw in the ice-core laboratory at the Desert Research Institute (DRI), Nevada.
steps.searchable_summary: Cut ice core samples for tephra analysis using a bandsaw

steps.description: Samples were transferred to precleaned Nalgene bottles and sent to Queen’s University Belfast (QUB) for tephra extraction where they were assigned unique identifier codes.
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer ice samples to Nalgene bottles and assign identifier codes

steps.description: The meltwater was centrifuged to concentrate particulates, which were then pipetted onto pre-ground glass slides that were placed on a hotplate in a laminar flow cupboard.
steps.searchable_summary: Centrifuge meltwater and pipette particulates onto glass slides

steps.description: The samples were covered in Buehler epoxy resin.
steps.searchable_summary: Cover samples with Buehler epoxy resin

steps.description: Tephra shard content was quantified using ×100–400 magnification on a light microscope with the aid of cross-polarization.
steps.searchable_summary: Quantify tephra shard content using light microscope

steps.description: Samples containing tephra were ground using 12 µm alumina powder and polished using 6, 3 and 1 µm diamond paste until the surfaces of the shards were exposed for analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Grind and polish samples to expose tephra shard surfaces

methods.description: Procedure for determining the source of tephra through major and trace element geochemical analysis.

steps.description: Major element geochemical analysis was performed on a JEOL FEGSEM 6500F electron microscope at QUB.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform major element geochemical analysis using JEOL FEGSEM

steps.description: Trace element composition was analyzed by laser ablation inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometry (LA-ICP-MS) on a Thermo SCIENTIFIC iCAP Q coupled to a Photon Machines Analyte Excite Excimer UV laser at the iCRAG Lab at Trinity College Dublin.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze trace element composition using LA-ICP-MS
